items.title: Under the old rules, let’s say the Jazz lose in the play-in next season, the Grizzlies would have 2% - 9% odds at a top-4 pick and 0% odds at picks 5-10
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Under the old rules, let’s say the Jazz lose in the play-in next season, the Grizzlies would have 2% - 9% odds at a top-4 pick and 0% odds at picks 5-10

Under new rules, the Grizzlies would have 0% odds of a top-5 pick but 15% - 28% odds at the sixth pick and 35% - 59% odds at landing a pick 6-10

items.title: Bonus Hollander O'Brien POTD Happy Hairston. NYU PF was drafted 35th by CIN & did a stint in DET but had his most famous years as a bookend to Wilt's title team in LA. Happy had 10 ppg 13 rpg in 74-75 but retired anyway at age 32. Died in 2001. RIP.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Bonus Hollander O'Brien POTD Happy Hairston. NYU PF was drafted 35th by CIN & did a stint in DET but had his most famous years as a bookend to Wilt's title team in LA. Happy had 10 ppg 13 rpg in 74-75 but retired anyway at age 32. Died in 2001. RIP.

items.title: In the calm before Game 7 bedlam, the Spurs' locker room rang out with Shaggy's 2000s hit song, "It Wasn't Me."
items.author: Jeff McDonald
items.body_excerpt:
In the calm before Game 7 bedlam, the Spurs' locker room rang out with Shaggy's 2000s hit song, "It Wasn't Me."

Meanwhile, the Spurs asked themselves a non-musical question: "Why not us?"

On a defiant Finals run, and a young team in no mood to wait its turn:

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: How the Spurs have arrived at the NBA Finals, well ahead of schedule
media.description: With a Finals date against New York set, Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs are bent on bucking basketball history

items.title: May 31, 1988: Magic Johnson with the no-look, over-the-shoulder pass to Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in Game 5 of the Western Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 31, 1988: Magic Johnson with the no-look, over-the-shoulder pass to Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in Game 5 of the Western Conference Finals.

Magic had 15 PTS/20 AST, Kareem had 21 PTS, and James Worthy scored 28 PTS in the Lakers' 119-102 win. Mark Aguirre scored 31 PTS for Dallas.

items.title: May 31, 1990: The Portland Trail Blazers advance to their second NBA Finals in franchise history with a 112-109 win over the Phoenix Suns in Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 31, 1990: The Portland Trail Blazers advance to their second NBA Finals in franchise history with a 112-109 win over the Phoenix Suns in Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals.

Clyde Drexler had 23 PTS/10 REB/7 AST and Terry Porter had 23 PTS/7 AST to lead the Blazers.
